title: mobile_scanner
objectives: Scan barcodes/QR quickly with ML Kit

sections.markdown: `mobile_scanner` is a lightweight, community-maintained barcode/QR scanner using Google ML Kit under the hood — much faster and more accurate than the older `qr_code_scanner`, with better lifecycle handling.

Real-world scanning UX needs **debouncing** (don't handle the same code 60 times a second) and clear scan-zone framing. Pass only the barcode formats you actually need; more formats = more CPU + more false positives.
Leaving the controller alive across screens → drains battery + keeps camera on. Dispose it in your State's dispose(), and pause on background.
For enterprise GS1/retail workflows (heavy throughput, damaged barcodes), Scandit outperforms ML Kit. mobile_scanner is the default for consumer/QR scanning; Scandit for pro workflows.

flashcards.back: Fewer formats = less CPU + fewer false positives.
onDetect fires continuously; debounce by rawValue + a cooldown timer.

quiz.explain: Scandit is tuned for tough workflows.

challenges.text: Scan a QR code and show its content.
Debounce duplicate reads and restrict to EAN-13 + QR.
Add scan-region overlay + torch toggle + camera switch.
Benchmark mobile_scanner vs Scandit on a set of damaged codes.
